title: AI Threats: Stay Safe Online

modules.title: How AI Changed the Threat Landscape â and Why You're a Target
modules.sub: Understanding why AI made online threats faster and more personal
modules.description: Learners explore how AI has shifted cybercrime from mass, generic attacks to highly targeted and automated ones. They examine why young adults are especially valuable targets, looking at the data, behaviors, and platforms that put them in the crosshairs.

modules.title: Deepfakes, Voice Clones, and AI-Powered Scams Targeting You
modules.sub: Recognizing synthetic media designed to deceive and manipulate you
modules.description: Learners analyze real-world examples of deepfake videos and AI voice clones used in scams, impersonation, and fraud. They practice spotting telltale signs of synthetic media and learn what to do when something — or someone — doesn't seem quite right.

modules.title: Phishing Got Smarter: Spotting AI-Generated Attacks
modules.sub: Detecting personalized, AI-crafted messages built to fool you
modules.description: Learners compare traditional phishing attempts with modern AI-generated ones that use personal data to sound convincing and legitimate. They work through realistic message examples to build the habit of pausing, questioning, and verifying before they click or respond.

modules.title: What AI Knows About You: Data, Profiles, and Who's Buying Them
modules.sub: Tracing the data trail you leave and who profits from it
modules.description: Learners map out the personal data they generate across apps, platforms, and devices, and discover how AI systems aggregate it into detailed profiles. They assess their own digital footprint and consider the real-world consequences of that information ending up in the wrong hands.

modules.title: Protecting Your Accounts and Devices in an AI-Powered World
modules.sub: Building a practical defense layer for your accounts and devices
modules.description: Learners apply concrete security practices — strong authentication, password hygiene, privacy settings, and device protections — to their actual accounts and devices. They make real configuration changes during the module, leaving with defenses that are already active rather than just planned.

modules.title: AI Tools That Defend You: What's Worth Using
modules.sub: Evaluating which AI-powered security tools actually earn your trust
modules.description: Learners survey the growing landscape of AI-driven security tools, from password managers to phishing detectors and identity monitors. They apply a practical evaluation framework to decide which tools are worth adopting, avoiding both false confidence and unnecessary complexity.

modules.title: Cybersecurity as a Career: How AI Is Reshaping the Field
modules.sub: Exploring the roles, skills, and opportunities AI is creating in security
modules.description: Learners get an honest look at how AI is transforming cybersecurity careers — eliminating some tasks while creating urgent demand for new roles and human judgment. They reflect on their own interests and strengths to consider whether a path in this field might be a fit for them.

modules.title: Your Personal Security Audit: Lock Things Down This Week
modules.sub: Running a real audit and acting on what you find right now
modules.description: Learners work through a structured personal security audit covering their accounts, devices, privacy settings, and digital exposure. They leave with a completed checklist, a prioritized action list, and the confidence to keep their security posture updated as threats continue to evolve.

synopsis: AI has made online scams, impersonation, and data harvesting dramatically more sophisticated â and most of the advice you've heard is already outdated. This course walks you through the real threats targeting people your age and gives you concrete steps to protect yourself, your identity, and your accounts.
rationale: Young adults are among the most targeted demographics for AI-powered scams and data exploitation, yet cybersecurity education rarely addresses the specific, AI-amplified risks they face in their daily digital lives.
learning_outcome: Learners can identify AI-powered threats like deepfake scams and intelligent phishing, complete a personal security audit, and put in place a practical set of defenses for their accounts and online presence.
source_signals: what type of cyberattack involves customizing a phishing message for a specific person
People tricking ChatGPT 'like watching an Asimov novel come to life'
